title: Ipomoea crispa
intro: Ipomoea crispa, also known as wild potato or sea potato, is a tropical vine native to the coastal regions of Southeast Asia and the Pacific Islands. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its numerous health benefits, including the treatment of digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Ipomoea crispa include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly prepared as an infusion, decoction, or dried powder, and is used in traditional herbal remedies for its purported health benefits.
